scope_note: Stanford's official first-year admissions pages do not publish a minimum GPA cutoff or admitted-student GPA distribution. This panel therefore summarizes what Stanford officially says about grades, course strength, and academic preparation instead of inventing a GPA threshold.
policy_summary: Stanford does not state a minimum required GPA on its first-year admissions pages. Instead, it tells applicants that courses and grades matter and evaluates them in the context of rigorous academic preparation.

what_mit_does_say.claim: Stanford says a strong academic foundation prepares students to thrive in its fast-paced and rigorous quarter system.
Stanford does not have a set of required courses for admission.
Stanford says students who demonstrate success and mastery in its recommended curriculum are prepared for the academic rigors at Stanford.
Stanford reviews applications holistically and says there are no minimum test scores required for admission and no score that guarantees admission.
